mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs, compressing corporate margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of observed cases, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 195.0 signals disinflationary pressure that reduces real yield expectations, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL falls below 195.0, or the signal loses predictive accuracy after 2006.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness that erodes consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% confirms macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values above 2.0% growth are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of quarters, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.15 over a decade.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index during a high-volatility regime reflects supply chain disruptions that trigger immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-volatility periods yield positive 5-day SPX returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical edge compared to a buy-and-hold baseline.
